title: Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs)
focus: Characteristics and diverse applications of Metal-Organic Frameworks.
rationale: MOFs are a highly examinable advanced material topic for UPSC Prelims (Science & Tech), focusing on their porous nature and specific environmental and industrial applications.
sectionExcerpt: MOFs are highly absorbent, porous materials inspired by diamond structures. They act as molecular storehouses capable of trapping specific gases, water, or chemicals. Key applications include water collection from dry air, extending food shelf life by absorbing ethylene gas, extracting pollutants like PFAS and antibiotics, mining rare-earth elements from wastewater, and storing gases like hydrogen and CO2.
evidenceBullets: MOFs are inspired by the structure of diamond and are more absorbent than zeolites.
They can capture water vapour directly from desert air.
In food processing, MOF-based packaging absorbs ethylene gas to increase shelf life.
MOFs are used to extract pollutants like PFAS and antibiotics from water, and to mine rare-earth elements from wastewater.
They act as molecular storehouses for gases such as hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and methane.

title: Electric Vehicles (EVs) and India Electric Mobility Index
focus: India Electric Mobility Index (IEMI), EV types, and government initiatives for EV adoption.
rationale: Covers the newly launched IEMI by NITI Aayog, basic EV technology types, and major government schemes (PM E-DRIVE, PLI), which are prime targets for policy and tech questions.
sectionExcerpt: NITI Aayog, with WRI India, launched the first India Electric Mobility Index (IEMI) to benchmark states' e-mobility progress across 16 indicators. Delhi topped the 2024 index. EVs include Battery EVs, Hybrid EVs, Fuel Cell EVs, and Plug-in Hybrids. India's EV push includes the PM E-DRIVE Scheme, PM e-Bus Sewa, PLI for Advanced Chemistry Cell (50 GWh), and the EVolutionS program by DST.
evidenceBullets: NITI Aayog and WRI India launched the first India Electric Mobility Index (IEMI).
IEMI tracks 16 indicators across Transport Electrification, Charging Infrastructure, and EV Research & Innovation.
Delhi topped the IEMI for 2024, followed by Maharashtra.
Fuel Cell EVs utilize fuel cell technology to generate electricity for propulsion.
The PM E-DRIVE Scheme is a Central Sector scheme supporting various e-vehicles and charging infrastructure.
The EVolutionS program for EV startups was launched by the Department of Science & Technology (DST).

title: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) Technology
focus: Concept and grid integration benefits of V2G technology.
rationale: V2G is an emerging tech concept linking EVs with renewable energy grid stability, making it a specific and highly testable concept for UPSC.
sectionExcerpt: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology enables EV batteries to send power back to the grid, acting as decentralized battery energy storage devices when not in use. A pilot project was initiated by KSEB and IIT Bombay. V2G facilitates the integration of Renewable Energy (RE), supports demand response markets, and improves grid stability by modulating power to reduce the impact of variable RE.
evidenceBullets: Kerala State Electricity Board (KSEB) and IIT Bombay initiated a pilot project for V2G technology.
V2G enables EV batteries to send power back to the grid, acting as decentralized energy storage.
It facilitates the integration of Renewable Energy (RE) and supports a demand response market.
V2G helps modulate power in EV batteries to improve grid stability against variable RE impacts.

title: Hyperloop Mobility System
focus: Functioning, components, and advantages of the Hyperloop system.
rationale: Hyperloop is a futuristic mobility tech. The focus is on its underlying principles (maglev, vacuum tubes, LIM) and recent developments in India.
sectionExcerpt: Maharashtra signed a deal with an IIT Madras startup to build a Linear Induction Motor (LIM)-based hyperloop connecting JNPT to Vadhavan Port. Hyperloop is a magnetic levitation (maglev) system where pods travel at ultra-high speeds (up to 1,200 km/h) through low-pressure sealed tubes. It utilizes vacuums, maglev for hovering, and LIMs for silent propulsion, offering energy-efficient and noise-reduced transport.
evidenceBullets: Maharashtra inked a deal with an IIT Madras startup to build a hyperloop connecting JNPT to Vadhavan Port.
Hyperloop uses a magnetic levitation (maglev) system inside low-pressure sealed tubes.
It utilizes Linear Induction Motors (LIM) for silent propulsion, enabling theoretical speeds of 1,200 km/h.
Key components include steel tubes, pressurized capsules, compressors, and air bearing suspension.

title: Deep Ocean Mission (DOM) and Samudrayaan
focus: India's deep-sea exploration capabilities and the upcoming Samudrayaan Mission.
rationale: Deep Ocean Mission is a flagship scientific initiative. The recent dives and international collaboration make it highly relevant for current affairs.
sectionExcerpt: The Samudrayaan Mission is slated for launch by late 2026 by the National Institute of Ocean Technology (NIOT). Recently, Indian aquanauts from NIOT dived over 5,000 meters in the Atlantic Ocean aboard the French submersible Nautile, in collaboration with IFREMER. This prelude to Samudrayaan places India among a select group of nations capable of deep-ocean crewed missions.
evidenceBullets: The Samudrayaan Mission will be launched by the end of 2026 by NIOT.
Indian aquanauts recently dived up to 5,002 meters in the Atlantic Ocean.
The dive was conducted aboard the French submersible Nautile in collaboration with France's IFREMER.
Only the U.S., Russia, China, France, and Japan have previously carried out successful deep-ocean crewed missions.
